article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: Classification direction and confidence are appropriate for a clearly right-leaning Fox News article with pro-parental-rights/local-control framing and no countervoices. However, the model cites at least one quoted phrase (e.g., 'restoring fundamental rights') as evidence, which is an attribution violation; some indicators are also vague.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Labeling as Right fits the one-sided framing, scare-quote usage, and lack of opposing perspectives; at worst it could be Lean Right, so within one class.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.92 is appropriate given strong, consistent signals and absence of balancing viewpoints.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Evidence mostly solid (authorial scare quotes, source/omission patterns), but it improperly relies on quoted language (e.g., 'restoring fundamental rights') and includes a vague DEI indicator.
